scope: Add a localized dispatch arm in detect_sinks_ast (crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs) for Ruby `subshell` nodes (backtick `…` and %x{…}/%x[…]/%x(…) forms). Treats subshell as a ShellExec sink; var-extraction reuses extract_first_identifier_arg_ast's generic descend-named-children path (subshell → interpolation → identifier). Adds 2 new fixtures (%x{} positive + %x{} string-literal FP) and 2 new test functions. Closes 1 carry-forward from vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket A Ruby subset (ruby_command_injection_positive). NO edits to ast_utils.rs (call_node_kinds / extract_call_name_ruby unchanged). NO new RUBY_AST_SINKS entry (would be dead code — subshell is not call-shaped).
closes_carry_forward: vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket A Ruby (1 test: ruby_command_injection_positive)

validator_mandates.ast_dispatch_localized_to_taint_rs: Single dispatch arm added to detect_sinks_ast (taint.rs:4256-4360). NO edits to crates/tldr-core/src/security/ast_utils.rs. The arm is keyed on `descendant.kind() == "subshell" && language == Language::Ruby` and bypasses the for-pattern-in-patterns.sinks loop entirely (subshell is not call-shaped, so no AstSinkPattern can match it via the existing call_names / member_patterns / raw-substring paths).
validator_mandates.no_call_node_kinds_extension: call_node_kinds(Ruby) at ast_utils.rs:18-36 stays as `["call", "method_call"]`. Adding `subshell` to the slice would propagate to detect_sources_ast (no Ruby source uses subshell — no value), the W2-pre call-shape path in member_patterns_match (would compare subshell text against member_patterns receiver/field — silent dead path or worse, weird matches), references.rs:3325 is_call gate (would mark subshells as calls in reference classification — semantic drift), and rr_baseline_per_language_test.rs:276 (which references RUBY_AST_SINKS `system` call_name shape — unaffected at HEAD, could drift). Localized dispatch arm avoids ALL of this.
validator_mandates.no_extract_call_name_extension: extract_call_name_ruby at ast_utils.rs:707-728 stays as `match node.kind() { "call" | "method_call" => …, _ => None }`. No subshell arm added. Returning a synthetic call name for subshell would pollute every consumer of extract_call_name (member_patterns_match call-shape path, find_parent_assignment_var, etc.) and is rejected.
validator_mandates.no_dead_bank_entries: NO new AstSinkPattern entry in RUBY_AST_SINKS (taint.rs:2758-2843). The existing for-loop dispatches via call_names (gated on call_node_kinds + extract_call_name) and member_patterns (gated on member_access_expression OR call-shape OR raw-substring). subshell satisfies none of these gates. An entry like `AstSinkPattern { call_names: &["`"], member_patterns: &[("", "`")], sink_type: ShellExec }` would be silently dead (matched by no path) and the raw-fallback `("", "`")` would FP on any line with a backtick character (e.g., docstrings). The dispatch arm IS the matcher.
validator_mandates.fp_regression_guard_preserved: Existing `ruby_command_injection_string_literal_fp` test asserts ZERO findings on a fixture that mentions the WORD 'backtick' inside string literals but contains NO real subshell node. The descendants-walk over its parsed AST yields zero `subshell` nodes, so the new dispatch arm cannot fire on it. Plus M2 adds a NEW `%x{...}` FP fixture (`command_injection_percent_x_string_literal_fp.rb`) asserting zero findings on a string mentioning `%x{cmd}` (no real subshell node).
validator_mandates.atomic_m2: M2 ships dispatch arm + 2 fixtures + 2 tests in ONE commit. atomic_commit:true; release_commit_group:milestone_2_atomic. M3 (CHANGELOG + tag) is a separate commit per precedent (vuln-source-parity-v1 M6 / sanitizer-removal-v1 M5).
validator_mandates.binary_smoke_required_in_m3: M3 produces `M3-binary-smoke.json` capturing `tldr vuln <fixture> --lang ruby --format json` output for both backtick and %x{} positive fixtures, asserting ≥1 finding of type `command_injection`. Catches any cargo-test-vs-binary drift (R3 risk in plan.md §7).
validator_mandates.node_audit_empirical: M1 produces `M1-node-audit.json` empirically verifying via tree-sitter-ruby 0.23.1 that BOTH `\`#{cmd}\`` and `%x{#{cmd}}` parse as a `subshell` named-node containing `interpolation` containing `identifier(cmd)`, and that `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast(subshell, source, Ruby)` returns `Some("cmd")`. If the helper returns `None`, M2 extends its body with a Ruby-specific subshell descent arm (still localized to taint.rs).
validator_mandates.predecessor_precedent_documented: Code comment at the dispatch arm AND CHANGELOG architectural-note BOTH cross-reference field_access_info-extension-v1's `\bgets\b` retained-carry-forward as the predecessor pattern (raw-substring/AST node-kind mismatch resolved via localized handling rather than cross-cutting helper extension).

staging_method.verification: git show HEAD --stat | grep -E 'continuum/autonomous/(?!ruby-backtick-extraction-v1-plan/)' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK
staging_method.rationale: Prevents accidental staging of unrelated continuum/autonomous artifacts; only this plan's files + the source code edits per milestone are eligible. Cargo.lock NEVER staged (`git checkout HEAD -- Cargo.lock` before each commit if dirty).

milestones.title: RED capture + tree-sitter-ruby node-kind audit + extract_first_identifier_arg_ast helper audit

red_tests.rationale: Already RED at HEAD `997557b`. M1 captures the RED output to lock the gating contract for M2 (RED→GREEN transition). NO new RED tests authored in M1 — the existing carry-forward test IS the contract.

green_files.purpose: Captures `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red ruby_command_injection_positive` RED output.

Empirical verification (via a one-off Rust test or script using tree-sitter-ruby 0.23.1) that: (1) `\`#{cmd}\`` parses as `subshell` containing `interpolation` containing `identifier(cmd)`; (2) `%x{#{cmd}}` parses as the same `subshell` kind; (3) `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast(subshell_node, source, Language::Ruby)` returns `Some("cmd")` for the canonical fixture. If (3) returns `None`, M2 must extend the helper with a Ruby-specific subshell descent arm — flagged in M1 report as a GATING decision for M2.

milestones.loc_delta_note: 0 source LOC; ~80 LOC across the 2 reports. M1 is purely investigative + documentation.
stop_thresholds: `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red ruby_command_injection_positive` REPORTS RED at HEAD (pre-M2). Captured in `M1-red-capture.txt`.
`M1-node-audit.json` confirms `subshell` kind for both backtick and %x{} shapes via empirical tree-sitter-ruby 0.23.1 parse.

`M1-node-audit.json` records the empirical verdict for `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast(subshell)` — either `"helper_returns_some"` (proceed with M2 as designed) or `"helper_returns_none"` (M2 must extend the helper; LOC delta upgraded from ~25 to ~40).

milestones.title: ATOMIC: Implement subshell dispatch arm in detect_sinks_ast + add 2 %x{} fixtures + 2 test functions

red_tests.rationale: ruby_command_injection_positive transitions RED → GREEN at M2 (carry-forward closure). The 2 NEW tests are authored in M2 and expected GREEN at M2-end (positive: ≥1 command_injection finding; FP: zero findings). ruby_command_injection_string_literal_fp (existing) REMAINS GREEN — regression-guard.

green_files.anchor: detect_sinks_ast at L4256-L4360 — immediately after the `for pattern in patterns.sinks { … }` block and BEFORE the closing brace of the `for descendant in &descendants` loop iteration
additions: ADD a dedicated dispatch arm gated on `language == Language::Ruby && descendant.kind() == "subshell"`. Var-extraction chain: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast → extract_assignment_rhs_ident → extract_source_var_from_statement (mirrors the var-extraction chain used elsewhere in detect_sinks_ast at L4316-L4334). On `Some(var)`, push `TaintSink { var, line, sink_type: TaintSinkType::ShellExec, statement: Some(stmt_text.to_string()) }` then `continue` to next descendant. On `None` (pure-static subshell with no interpolation), emit nothing — correct (no taint flow possible).

ADD ~10-line comment block at the arm citing: (a) the carry-forward source (vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket A Ruby), (b) tree-sitter-ruby's collapse of backtick + %x{} onto subshell, (c) the FAI-v1 \bgets\b precedent, (d) Option A (call_node_kinds extension) explicitly rejected with rationale — see plan.md §5 for full comment text.
preserved: RUBY_AST_SINKS at taint.rs:2758-2843 unchanged (no new AstSinkPattern entry)

green_files.purpose: NEW positive fixture exercising the %x{...} form. ~7 lines: class with handler that takes params[:cmd] and runs `%x{#{cmd}}`. Asserts the dispatch arm covers BOTH lexical forms (backtick + %x{}) via the single `subshell` node-kind.
NEW FP regression-guard fixture mentioning the WORD `%x{cmd}` inside a string literal but containing NO real subshell node. Asserts zero findings.

green_files.anchor: around L988 (after existing ruby_command_injection_string_literal_fp at L990-L1000)
additions: ADD `ruby_command_injection_percent_x_positive` test function — mirrors `ruby_command_injection_positive` shape (asserts ≥1 finding of type command_injection).
ADD `ruby_command_injection_percent_x_string_literal_fp` test function — mirrors the existing FP guard (asserts zero findings).

milestones.loc_delta_note: Source-only ~25 LOC (dispatch arm + comment); fixtures ~14 LOC; test functions ~20 LOC; total ~60 LOC. If R1 triggers (extract_first_identifier_arg_ast extension required), source-only bumps to ~40 LOC, total ~75 LOC — still inside the §0 LOC bound.
stop_thresholds: `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red ruby_command_injection_string_literal_fp` GREEN (regression-guard preserved)
Staged files limited to: taint.rs + 2 ruby fixtures + vuln_migration_v1_red.rs + this plan dir's milestone reports. Cargo.lock NOT staged.
milestones.rollback_rule: If any post-commit assertion fails, REVERT the M2 atomic commit and re-investigate. NO partial-fix follow-up commits.

milestones.title: Verification + CHANGELOG entry + local annotated tag

green_files.purpose: Internal-milestone entry per vuln-source-parity-v1 M6 / sanitizer-removal-v1 M5 / field_access_info-extension-v1 M6 precedent
sections: ### Added: AST dispatch arm in detect_sinks_ast for Ruby subshell nodes (backtick + %x{} forms). Treats subshell as ShellExec sink; var-extraction reuses extract_first_identifier_arg_ast. Closes 1 carry-forward from vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket A Ruby (ruby_command_injection_positive). Adds 2 new fixtures covering the %x{} shape.

### Architectural note: Localized dispatch arm rather than call_node_kinds(Ruby) extension. Avoids polluting cross-cutting helpers (call_node_kinds, extract_call_name_ruby) and their consumers (sources, sanitizers, references.rs is_call). Predecessor pattern reference: field_access_info-extension-v1 retained \bgets\b for the bare-call AST shape gap — same shape of carry-forward, different localized resolution.
### Retained: call_node_kinds(Ruby) unchanged; extract_call_name_ruby unchanged; RUBY_AST_SINKS unchanged (no new AstSinkPattern); public API unchanged.

green_files.purpose: Captures `tldr vuln <fixture> --lang ruby --format json` output for both backtick and %x{} positive fixtures. Asserts ≥1 finding of type `command_injection` for each. Catches any cargo-test-vs-binary drift (R3 risk in plan.md §7).

Summary: vuln_migration_v1_red count delta (158/166 → 161/168 with 2 new tests added; or 159/166 if measured against original suite size); workspace test count delta; LOC delta from `git diff HEAD~N --stat`; tag confirmation.

stop_thresholds: `tldr vuln crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/ruby/command_injection_positive.rb --lang ruby --format json` returns ≥1 command_injection finding.
`tldr vuln crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/ruby/command_injection_percent_x_positive.rb --lang ruby --format json` returns ≥1 command_injection finding.
Local annotated tag `ruby-backtick-extraction-v1` applied at HEAD post-M3.
NO push, NO publish, NO version bump.

per_form_verdict.backtick_no_interpolation: GREEN-no-emit — pure-static `\`ls\`` has no interpolation, extract_first_identifier_arg_ast returns None, no sink emitted. Correct (no taint flow possible).
per_form_verdict.backtick_with_interpolation: GREEN — primary closure target. `\`#{cmd}\`` parses as subshell→interpolation→identifier(cmd). Helper extracts `cmd`, dispatch arm emits ShellExec sink. Closes ruby_command_injection_positive.
per_form_verdict.percent_x_with_interpolation: GREEN — secondary closure target. `%x{#{cmd}}` parses as subshell→interpolation→identifier(cmd). Same dispatch arm, same outcome. Locked in by NEW M2 fixture `command_injection_percent_x_positive.rb`.
per_form_verdict.percent_x_brackets_or_parens: GREEN — all 6 lexical forms (`…`, %x{…}, %x[…], %x(…), and 2 less-common variants) collapse onto the same subshell node-kind in tree-sitter-ruby 0.23.1.
per_form_verdict.subshell_in_heredoc: OUT-OF-SCOPE — no carry-forward test demands; no fixture; not investigated.

investigation_findings.consequence: detect_sinks_ast call_names path AND member_patterns_match call-shape path BOTH gate on extract_call_name(...).is_some() — both return early on subshell. for-pattern-in-patterns.sinks loop cannot match subshell via any structural shape. raw-substring fallback would require a member_patterns entry like ("", "`") — high FP risk and rejected. Dedicated dispatch arm IS required.
investigation_findings.is_in_string_safety_for_subshell: subshell is NOT a string kind. is_in_string walking up from subshell returns false. The descendants-loop's is_in_string skip will NOT filter subshell or its inner identifier inside `#{...}` interpolation. Verified by reading is_in_string at ast_utils.rs:592-602.
investigation_findings.fp_fixture_safety: command_injection_string_literal_fp.rb mentions WORD 'backtick cmd' inside a string but contains NO real `…` subshell node. Descendants walk over its parsed AST yields zero subshell nodes — zero FP regression.
investigation_findings.extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_verdict: GENERIC PATH (post all language-specific shortcut arms at taint.rs:3934-3982) walks named descendants of the input node looking for an identifier. For subshell containing `#{cmd}`, the walk yields subshell→interpolation→identifier(cmd). Empirical verification deferred to M1 node-audit; if the helper fails on subshell, M2 extends it with a Ruby-specific arm (still localized to taint.rs).

side_effect_audit_summary.sources: RUBY_AST_SOURCES has ("", "params[") raw-fallback. Subshell text containing params[...] would already match this at HEAD. Out of scope; canonical fixture uses cmd = params[:cmd]; \`#{cmd}\` so source detection fires on the assignment, not the subshell.
side_effect_audit_summary.sanitizers: RUBY_AST_SANITIZERS gates on call_node_kinds — unchanged → unaffected.
side_effect_audit_summary.references_rs: is_call check at references.rs:3325 consumes call_node_kinds — unchanged → unaffected.
side_effect_audit_summary.rr_baseline_per_language_test: Tests RUBY_AST_SINKS `system` call_name — RUBY_AST_SINKS unchanged → unaffected.
side_effect_audit_summary.rr_module_function_integ_test: References member_patterns_match call-shape path — call_node_kinds(Ruby) unchanged → unaffected.

options_considered.rejected_reason: Insufficient — extract_call_name_ruby returns None for subshell, so call_names path still cannot fire. Plus broad side-effect surface (sources, sanitizers, references.rs).

options_considered.summary: Add `subshell` to call_node_kinds(Ruby) AND extend extract_call_name_ruby with synthetic name
options_considered.rejected_reason: Same broad side-effect surface. Synthetic call name pollutes downstream consumers. Cross-cutting change to ast_utils.rs when a localized change in taint.rs suffices.

options_considered.summary: Dedicated dispatch arm in detect_sinks_ast keyed on `descendant.kind() == "subshell" && language == Ruby`
options_considered.rationale: Localized to taint.rs. No cross-cutting helper change. No call_node_kinds extension → no side-effect on sources/sanitizers/references. Reuses existing extract_first_identifier_arg_ast helper. LOC delta ~10-25.

premortem_risks.R1_helper_doesnt_descend_through_interpo: MEDIUM — extract_first_identifier_arg_ast generic body may not walk through `interpolation` named-children. Mitigated by M1 empirical verification; if helper fails, M2 extends it (LOC bumps ~25 → ~40, still in bound).
premortem_risks.R2_side_effect_on_ruby_source_detection: LOW — RUBY_AST_SOURCES ("", "params[") raw-fallback already exists; subshell containing params[...] would match it pre- and post-milestone alike. Documented; out of scope.
premortem_risks.R3_parallel_sink_dispatch_loop_missed: MEDIUM-LOW — currently no parallel build_sinks_ast_index helper exists (sinks dispatch flows through detect_sinks_ast exclusively). M3 binary-smoke gate catches drift. Future milestones that add such a helper own the responsibility of replicating this arm.
premortem_risks.R4_tree_sitter_ruby_version_drift: LOW — Cargo.lock pinned to 0.23.1. Future version-bump PRs must re-verify the M1 node-audit.
premortem_risks.R5_raw_substring_fp_class_via_backtick_p: LOW — rejected by design. Option B uses node-kind dispatch, not raw-substring pattern.

pipeline_metadata.predecessor: vuln-source-parity-v1 (HEAD 997557b transitively includes the merged tag)
